stages.name: unit_tests
stages.evidence: test/sandbox_test.zig 'F-1: stable-operator Mutant.original survives parse teardown' drives the real pipeline (parse -> collect -> finish -> parsed.deinit() -> sandbox.apply) for error_catch_unreachable, boolean_literal, and integer_literal_boundary, parsing with the poisoning std.testing.allocator and storing candidates in a separate long-lived collector arena. Before the fix it crashed/failed at sandbox_test.zig:169 (the post-deinit `expectEqualStrings(original)` read the freed owned_source, poisoned to 0xAA): 505/506 tests passed, 1 crashed. After the fix the collector owns `original`, so all three operators apply cleanly: 506/506 green. test/integration_run_test.zig asserts the built binary reports total=3 killed=1 survived=2 invalid=0 and that the error_catch_unreachable entry is `survived` with original `0`.

stages.name: real_binary_behavior
stages.command: zig build -Doptimize=<mode> && ./zig-out/bin/zentinel --config zentinel.toml run --report json  (over the integration fixture)
stages.evidence: Before the fix the Debug binary reported error_catch_unreachable -> invalid with original=[170] (the 0xAA poison byte): summary survived=1 invalid=1, hiding the real survivor. After the fix the verdict is identical across all four optimize modes -- Debug, ReleaseSafe, ReleaseFast, ReleaseSmall each report total=3 killed=1 survived=2 invalid=0, with error_catch_unreachable -> survived and original='0'. This proves the dangling-original misclassification (and its Debug/ReleaseSafe-vs-ReleaseFast nondeterminism) is resolved end-to-end.

stages.name: mutation_checks
stages.reason: Task 117 fixes a deterministic-core defect in candidate ownership; the behavioral fix is proven by a failing-before/passing-after unit test plus a real-binary before/after verdict across four optimize modes. Full self-mutation dogfood remains gated to task 085.

residual_risk: The fix dupes `original` for every candidate in ast_backend.Collector.add, so all stable operators (including the source-slice operators that were safe only by accident) now own their `original` bytes uniformly; the read happens while `parsed` is alive, so the captured text is always correct. `original` is the only Mutant field that borrowed the parsed tree -- `file` is the caller's long-lived slice and `replacement`/`operator`/`backend_version`/`equivalent_risks` are static or already collector-allocator-owned.
The duped bytes live in the collector allocator (the run arena in production), freed once at run teardown; there is no double free with parsed.deinit(), which only frees owned_source/file/diags/tree.

`invalid` stays reserved for genuine I-011 contract violations (malformed span, span/original length mismatch, no-op replacement); a candidate with a valid patch is never silently dropped to `invalid`. The sandbox PatchMismatch path still guards against true span/source disagreement.

The integration fixture now enables error_catch_unreachable and covers parsePositive only on its success path, so a real survivor (untested error path) is reported rather than hidden; full per-operator blast-radius coverage for the remaining Phase-2 operators stays in their own mutator unit tests.
